---
jupyter: python3
format: 
  pdf:
    echo: false
    geometry:
      - top=5mm        
      - left=5mm
      - right=5mm
      - bottom=20mm
      - headheight=35mm 
      - headsep=5mm 
      - includehead=true 
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage{multirow}
          \usepackage{graphicx}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{fancyhdr}
          \usepackage{lastpage}
          \usepackage{multicol}

          \pagestyle{fancy}
          \fancyhf{} 
          \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
          

          \fancyhead[C]{
            \noindent
            \bgroup
            \def\arraystretch{1.4}
            \begin{tabularx}{\textwidth}{|p{5.5cm}|p{1.8cm}|X|p{1.2cm}|p{1.8cm}|}
            \hline
            \multirow{5}{5.5cm}{
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \includegraphics[width=4.5cm]{logo} \\
                \fontsize{6pt}{7pt}\selectfont
                \textbf{CIVIL \& ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING,}\\
                SKEMPTON BUILDING, IMPERIAL COLLEGE\\
                LONDON, SOUTH KENSINGTON CAMPUS,\\
                LONDON SW7 2AZ.\\[3mm]
                \normalsize\textbf{CALCULATION SHEET}
            }
            & \textbf{Job No.} & & \textbf{Sheet} & \textbf{Ref} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \textbf{Job Title} & Steel Design Coursework & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\thepage\ of \pageref{LastPage}} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multicolumn{4}{p{\dimexpr\textwidth-5.5cm-8\tabcolsep-5\arrayrulewidth\relax}|}{
                \textbf{Subject} \newline
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \centerline{Bracing Members Calculations}
                \vspace*{1mm}
            } \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multirow{2}{*}{\textbf{Client}} & \textbf{Made by} \quad Pablo Peng & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date} \quad 03/03/2026} \\ \cline{3-5}
            & & \textbf{Checked by} Vincent Chan \quad & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date}} \\ \hline
            \end{tabularx}
            \egroup
          }
---

\Large 
\linespread{1.4}
\selectfont 

```{python}
# Define variables
import math
section = '356 x 406x 287'
lt = math.sqrt(6000**2+8250**2)
lc = math.sqrt(4000**2+8250**2)
Nedd = 1611
Nedv = 3008
Ntedd = 2107
Ntedv = 4247
area_reduction = 0.7
# setup
%precision 2
import pandas as pd
import math

df_all = pd.read_csv('section_mm.csv')
if df_all.columns[0] == 'Unnamed: 0':
  df_all = df_all.rename(columns = {'Unnamed: 0' : 'Section Profile'})

# Scientific notation formatter
def sci(value):
    exp_num = math.floor(math.log10(abs(value)))
    base = value / (10 ** exp_num)
    return value, base, exp_num

#material
fu= 500.0
fy = 355.0
E = 210000
G = 81000

df = df_all[df_all['Section Profile'] == section]
#section
h = float(df['h (mm)'].iloc[0])
b = float(df['b (mm)'].iloc[0])
tw = float(df['tw (mm)'].iloc[0])
tf = float(df['tf (mm)'].iloc[0])
d = float(df['d (mm)'].iloc[0])
r= float(df['r(mm)'].iloc[0])
A, A1, A2 = sci(float(df['A (mm^2)'].iloc[0]))
Iy, Iy1, Iy2 = sci(float(df['Iy (mm^4)'].iloc[0]))
Iz, Iz1, Iz2 = sci(float(df['Iz (mm^4)'].iloc[0]))
Ry = float(df['Ry (mm)'].iloc[0])
Rz = float(df['Rz (mm)'].iloc[0])
Wey, Wey1, Wey2 = sci(float(df['W_el,y (mm^3)'].iloc[0]))
Wez, Wez1, Wez2 = sci(float(df['W_el,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0]))
Wpy, Wpy1, Wpy2 = sci(float(df['W_pl,y (mm3)'].iloc[0]))
Wpz, Wpz1, Wpz2 = sci(float(df['W_pl,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0]))
Iw, Iw1, Iw2 = sci(float(df['I_w (mm^6)'].iloc[0]))
It, It1, It2 = sci(float(df['I_T (mm^4)'].iloc[0]))

ratio = h/b
if ratio > 1.2:
  if tf <= 40:
    ay = 0.21
    az = 0.34
  else:
    ay = 0.34
    az = 0.49
else:
  if tf <= 100:
    ay = 0.34
    az = 0.49
  else:
    ay = 0.70
    az = 0.70
```

#
# Effective length of column about y-y

\begin{description}
  \item[]Ultimate strength, $f_{u}$ = `{python} fy` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Yield strength, $f_{y}$ = `{python} fu` $N \cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Young's modulus, $E$ = `{python} E` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
\end{description}

# Section Properties of `{python} section`
\begin{multicols}{2}
\begin{description}
  \item[] Overall depth, $h$ = `{python} h` mm  
  \item[] Width of section, $b$ = `{python} b` mm  
  \item[] Web thickness, $t_{w}$ = `{python} tw` mm  
  \item[] Flange thickness, $t_{f}$ = `{python} tf` mm  
  \item[] Depth between fillet, $h_{w}$ = `{python} d` mm  
  \item[] Root radius, $r$ = `{python} r` mm  
  \item[] Area of section, $A$ = `{python} A1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} A2`}$ $mm^{2}$ 
  \item[] 2nd moment Y-Y, $I_{y}$ = `{python} Iy1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Iy2`}$ $mm^{4}$  
  \item[] 2nd moment Z-Z, $I_{z}$ = `{python} Iz1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Iz2`}$ $mm^{4}$  
  \item[] Radius of gyration Y-Y, $i_{y}$ = `{python} Ry` mm  
  \item[] Radius of gyration Z-Z, $i_{z}$ = `{python} Rz` mm  
  \item[] Elastic modulus Y-Y, $W_{el,y}$ = `{python} Wey1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wey2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Elastic modulus Z-Z, $W_{el,z}$ = `{python} Wez1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wez2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Plastic modulus Y-Y, $W_{pl,y}$ = `{python} Wpy1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wpy2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Plastic modulus Z-Z, $W_{pl,z}$ = `{python} Wpz1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wpz2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Warping constant, $I_{w}$ = `{python} Iw1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Iw2`}$ $mm^{6}$  
  \item[] Torsional constant, $I_{t}$ = `{python} It1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} It2`}$   $mm^{4}$  
\end{description}
\end{multicols}

```{python}
ep = math.sqrt(235/fy)
crf = 9*ep
crw = 33*ep
cw = d
c1 = cw/tw
cf = (b-tw-2*r)/2
c2 = cf/tf
```

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
# Length of critical diagonal columns
\begin{description}
  \item[]Length of compressive critical diagonal column, $L_{c}$ = $\sqrt{6000^2 + 8250^2}$ = `{python} lc` mm
  \item[]Length of tensile critical diagonal column, $L_{t}$ = $\sqrt{4000^2 + 8250^2}$ = `{python} lt` mm
\end{description}

# Section Classification

According to EN 1993-1-1:2022 Eq 5.1, $\epsilon$ = $\sqrt{\frac{235}{f_{y}}}$ = `{python} ep` 

\begin{multicols}{2}
\begin{description}
  \item[]$c_{w}$ = $\frac{(b-t_{w}-2\cdot r)}{2}$ = `{python} cw`  
  \item[] $\frac{c_{w}}{t_{w}}$ = `{python} c1`
  \item[]Class 1 limit for web, 72 $\epsilon $ = `{python} crw`
  \item[]$c_{f}$ = $h_{w}$ = `{python} cf` 
  \item[]$\frac{c_{f}}{t_{f}}$ = `{python} c2`
  \item[]Class 1 limit for flange, 9 $\epsilon$ = `{python} crf`
\end{description}  
\end{multicols}

Since `{python} c1` < `{python} crw` and `{python} c2` < `{python} crf`, the section belongs to class 1 
 

```{python}
def buckle (Lcr, I, E, A, fy, a):
  Ncr = (math.pi**2 * E * I) / (Lcr**2)/1000
  lam = math.sqrt(( A*fy / (Ncr*1000) ))
  phi = 0.5*(1 + a*(lam - 0.2) + lam**2)
  chi = min(1/(phi + math.sqrt(phi**2 - lam**2)),1)
  return Ncr, lam, ratio, phi, chi

Lcr = lc
Ncry, lamy, ratio, phiy, chiy = buckle(Lcr, Iy, E, A, fy, ay)
Ncrz, lamz, ratio, phiz, chiz = buckle(Lcr, Iz, E, A, fy, az)

chi = min([chiy,chiz])
Nrd = chi*A*fy/1000

if Nrd > max(Nedd, Nedv):
  outputn='the section is safe in compression'
else:
  outputn='the section is not safe in compression'
```

# Buckling resistance about y-y axis

\begin{description}
  \item[]$L_{cr,y}$ = $L_c$ =`{python} Lcr` mm
  \item[]$N_{cr,y}$ = $\frac{\pi^{2}\cdot E\cdot I_{y}}{{L_{cr,y}}^{2}}$ = `{python} Ncry` kN
  \item[]$\lambda_{y}$ =  $\sqrt{\frac{N_{Rk}}{N_{cr,y}}}$ = $\sqrt{\frac{A\cdot f_{y}}{N_{cr,y}}}$ = `{python} lamy` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.69)
  \item[]$\frac{h}{b}$ = `{python} ratio` < 1.2
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
\begin{description}
  \item[]$t_{f}$ = `{python} tf` mm < 100~mm
  \item[]Use buckling curve B,  $\alpha_{y}$ = `{python} ay` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Table 8.2)
  \item[]$\phi_{y}$ = $0.5 \cdot (1 + \alpha_{y} \cdot (\lambda_{y} - 0.2) + {\lambda_{y}}^{2})$ = `{python} phiy` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.74)
  \item[]$\chi_{y}$ = $\frac{1}{\phi_{y} + \sqrt{{\phi_{y}}^{2} - {\lambda_{y}}^{2}}}$ = `{python} chiy` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.73)
\end{description}
 
# Buckling resistance about z-z axis
\begin{description}
  \item[]$L_{cr,z}$ = `{python} Lcr` mm
  \item[]$N_{cr,z}$ = $\frac{\pi^{2}\cdot E\cdot I_{z}}{{L_{cr,z}}^{2}}$ = `{python} Ncrz` kN
  \item[]$\lambda_{z}$ = $\sqrt{\frac{N_{Rk}}{N_{cr,z}}}$ = $\sqrt{\frac{A\cdot f_{y}}{N_{cr,z}}}$ = `{python} lamz` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.69)
  \item[]$\frac{h}{b}$ = `{python} ratio` < 1.2
  \item[]$t_{f}$ = `{python} tf` mm < 100~mm
  \item[]Use buckling curve C,  $\alpha_{z}$ = `{python} az` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Table 8.2)
  \item[]$\phi_{z}$ = $0.5 \cdot (1 + \alpha_{z} \cdot (\lambda_{z} - 0.2) + {\lambda_{z}}^{2})$ = `{python} phiz` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.74)
  \item[]$\chi_{z}$ = $\frac{1}{\phi_{z} + \sqrt{{\phi_{z}}^{2} - {\lambda_{z}}^{2}}}$ = `{python} chiz` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.73)
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
# Buckling resistance
\begin{description}
  \item[]$\chi$ = min($\chi_{y},\chi_{z}$) = `{python} chi`
  \item[]$N_{rd}$ = $\chi\cdot A\cdot f_{y}$ = `{python} Nrd` kN --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.68)
  \item[]$N_{ed}$ for diagonal members = `{python} Nedd` kN.
  \item[]$N_{ed}$ for vertical members = `{python} Nedv` kN.
  \item[]Therefore `{python} outputn`
\end{description}


```{python}
gamma0 = 1.0
gamma1 = 1.25
Nplrd = A*fy/gamma0/1000
Nurd = area_reduction*A*fu/gamma1/1000
Ntrd = min(Nplrd, Nurd)
if Ntrd > max(Ntedd, Ntedv):
  outputt='the section is safe in tension'
else:
  outputt='the section is not safe in tension'
```

# Tension resistance of diagonal members

\begin{description}
  \item[]Gross area tension resistance :
  \item[]$N_{pl,RD}$ = $\frac{A\cdot f_{y}}{\gamma_{1}}$ = `{python} Nplrd` kN --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.14)
  \item[]Assume the net area is 70\% of the gross area and smooth holes, k = 1
  \item[]Net area tension resistance :
  \item[]$N_{u,RD}$ = `{python} area_reduction` $\cdot\frac{k\cdot A_{net}\cdot f_{u}}{\gamma_{0}}$ = `{python} Nurd` kN --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.15)
  \item[]$N_{t,rd}$ = min($N_{pl,RD}$, $N_{u,RD}$) = `{python} Ntrd` kN
  \item[]$N_{t,ed}$ for diagonal members = `{python} Ntedd` kN.
  \item[]$N_{t,ed}$ for vertical members = `{python} Ntedv` kN.
  \item[]Therefore `{python} outputt`
\end{description}
